2605.00424.title: Skills as Verifiable Artifacts: A Trust Schema and a Biconditional Correctness Criterion for Human-in-the-Loop Agent Runtimes
2605.00424.paper_summary: Agent skills -- structured packages of instructions, scripts, and references that augment a large language model (LLM) without modifying the model itself -- have moved from convenience to first-class deployment artifact. The runtime that loads them inherits the same problem package managers and operating systems have always faced: a piece of content claims a behavior; the runtime must decide whether to believe it. We argue this paper's central thesis up front: a skill is \emph{untrusted code} until it is verified, and the runtime that loads it must enforce that default rather than infer trust from a signature, a clearance, or a registry of origin. Without skill verification, a human-in-the-loop (HITL) gate must fire on every irreversible call -- which is operationally untenable and degrades into rubber-stamping at any non-trivial scale. With skill verification treated as a separate, gated process, HITL fires only for what is unverified, and the system becomes sustainable. We give a trust schema (§\ref{sec:schema}) that includes an explicit verification level on every skill manifest; a capability gate (§\ref{sec:gate}) whose HITL policy is a function of that verification level; a \emph{biconditional} correctness criterion (§\ref{sec:biconditional}) that any candidate verification procedure must satisfy on an adversarial-ensemble exercise (§\ref{sec:eval}); and a portable runtime profile (§\ref{sec:guidelines}) with ten normative guidelines abstracted from a working open-source reference implementation \cite{metere2026enclawed}. The contribution is harness- and model-agnostic; nothing here requires retraining, fine-tuning, or proprietary infrastructure.
2605.00424.paper_summary_zh: 代理技能——結構化的指令包、腳本和參考資料，能增強大型語言模型（LLM）而不修改模型本身——已經從便利性轉變為一流的部署工件。加載這些技能的運行時繼承了包管理器和操作系統一直面臨的相同問題：一段內容聲稱一種行為；運行時必須決定是否相信它。我們在前面提出這篇論文的中心論點：技能是\emph{不受信任的代碼}，直到它被驗證，而加載它的運行時必須強制執行這一默認，而不是從簽名、許可或來源登記中推斷信任。沒有技能驗證，每一次不可逆的調用都必須啟動人類介入（HITL）閘門——這在操作上是不可行的，並且在任何非微不足道的規模上都會退化為橡皮圖章。將技能驗證視為一個單獨的、有門檻的過程，HITL僅在未經驗證的情況下啟動，系統變得可持續。我們提供了一個信任架構（§\ref{sec:schema}），其中包括每個技能清單上的明確驗證級別；一個能力閘（§\ref{sec:gate}），其HITL政策是該驗證級別的函數；一個\emph{雙條件}正確性標準（§\ref{sec:biconditional}），任何候選驗證程序必須在對抗性集合演練中滿足（§\ref{sec:eval}）；以及一個可攜帶的運行時配置文件（§\ref{sec:guidelines}），其中包含十條從一個運行中的開源參考實現中抽象出的規範指導方針\cite{metere2026enclawed}。這一貢獻是與束和模型無關的；這裡的任何內容都不需要重新訓練、微調或專有基礎設施。
2605.00424.author: Alfredo Metere et.al.
